final_prompt: Eye-level photograph of a sunlit indoor still life featuring exactly two distinct bouquets of roses on a rustic wooden table. The table, characterized by warm brown tones, visible grain, and knots, occupies the bottom 50% of the frame. Spatial arrangement: A bouquet of deep red roses with green leaves is positioned strictly on the left side, while a separate bouquet of light pink and mauve roses with green foliage is positioned strictly on the right side. A clear, empty gap separates the two arrangements; no ribbons or ties are present. Lighting: Bright natural sunlight streams from a window in the left background, casting sharp, diagonal shadows of both bouquets across the wood surface toward the right. Background: A soft, out-of-focus beige wall with a bright white window frame visible on the left. Atmosphere: Warm and serene, highlighting velvety petal textures and wood grain.
scene_prompt: An eye-level photograph of a sunlit indoor scene featuring two separate, independent bouquets of roses resting on a rustic wooden table. The table has a warm brown finish with visible wood grain and knots, occupying the lower half of the frame. On the left side of the table stands a bouquet composed primarily of deep red roses with green leaves, and on the right side stands a second bouquet featuring lighter pink and mauve roses, also with green foliage. Unlike the original single arrangement, these two bunches are distinct and separated by a small gap, with no ribbon tying them together. Sunlight streams in strongly from a window located on the left side of the frame (background), casting sharp, diagonal shadows of the two separate bouquets onto the wooden surface towards the right. The background consists of a soft, out-of-focus beige wall and the bright white frame of the window on the left. The atmosphere is warm and serene, highlighting the velvety texture of the flower petals and the natural grain of the wood. Perspective: Camera viewpoint: Eye-level view facing the bouquet directly; Spatial positions: Bouquet is in the CENTER foreground; Window is in the LEFT background; Wooden table occupies the BOTTOM half; Dominant color palette: Deep reds and pinks (flowers), dark green (ribbon/leaves), warm brown (table), soft white/beige (background); Lighting quality + direction: Bright natural sunlight streaming from the LEFT, casting distinct shadows to the RIGHT. Style reference: Dominant color palette: Deep reds and pinks (flowers), dark green (ribbon/leaves), warm brown (table), soft white/beige (background); Dominant color palette: Deep reds and pinks (flowers), dark green (ribbon/leaves), warm brown (table), soft white/beige (background); Spatial positions: Bouquet is in the CENTER foreground; Window is in the LEFT background; Wooden table occupies the BOTTOM half; Overall atmosphere: Warm, serene, sunlit indoor still life.
reasoning_chain: Step 1: Observe the current state: a single bouquet of mixed red and pink roses tied with a green ribbon, standing on a wooden table near a window.

Step 2: Infer the counterfactual change: The instruction asks to split this single arrangement into two independent bouquets. This implies physically separating the stems into two distinct groups and removing or adjusting the binding ribbon so they stand apart.

Step 3: Derive the visual outcome: Two separate bunches of roses will occupy the space previously held by one. They should be positioned side-by-side (e.g., left and right) on the same wooden surface, maintaining the original lighting and background context.
instruction: 如果这一束花分成两束独立的玫瑰，会是什么样子？
visual_cues: Camera viewpoint: Eye-level view facing the bouquet directly.
Spatial positions: Bouquet is in the CENTER foreground; Window is in the LEFT background; Wooden table occupies the BOTTOM half.
Dominant color palette: Deep reds and pinks (flowers), dark green (ribbon/leaves), warm brown (table), soft white/beige (background).
Lighting quality + direction: Bright natural sunlight streaming from the LEFT, casting distinct shadows to the RIGHT.
Surface materials/textures: Rustic wood grain on the table, velvety texture of rose petals, smooth satin ribbon.
Overall atmosphere: Warm, serene, sunlit indoor still life.
physics_implications: The single central mass splits into two smaller masses.
The binding ribbon is removed or separated, no longer holding all stems together.
Shadows on the table will shift from a single central shadow to two distinct shadows corresponding to the new positions of the bouquets.
The total volume of flowers remains constant, just distributed differently.

vqa_checklist.q: Are there exactly two separate bouquets of roses on the table?
Is the green ribbon that originally tied the single bouquet absent or clearly not binding both groups together?
Does the lighting still come from the left side, casting shadows to the right?
Is the wooden table and window background preserved from the original scene?
